symbols.name: m
symbols.def: number of non-collinear latent confounders

symbols.name: n
symbols.def: n=m+2

symbols.name: L
symbols.def: truncation endpoint

symbols.name: r,a
symbols.def: cumulant order and number of Y arguments

symbols.name: i,j
symbols.def: latent-loading and source indices

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: observational law of (X,Y)

symbols.name: X,Y
symbols.def: observed pair with joint law P

symbols.name: S_j
symbols.def: centered independent source indexed by j

symbols.name: gamma
symbols.def: forward direct-effect slope

symbols.name: rho_i
symbols.def: forward latent-source slope

symbols.name: delta
symbols.def: reverse direct-effect slope

symbols.name: sigma_i
symbols.def: reverse latent-source slope

symbols.name: u_j
symbols.def: u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j) for 1<=j<=m, and u_{m+1}=(0,1)

symbols.name: v_j
symbols.def: v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1) for 1<=j<=m, and v_{m+1}=(delta,1)

symbols.name: c_{jr},d_{jr}
symbols.def: r-th source cumulants in the forward and reverse parameterizations

symbols.name: kappa_{r,a}(P)
symbols.def: Cum_P(X repeated r-a times,Y repeated a times)

symbols.name: T_L(P),t
symbols.def: T_L(P)=(kappa_{r,a}(P):2<=r<=L,0<=a<=r) in lexicographic order, and t is a vector in the corresponding complex coordinate space

symbols.name: Theta^right_{m,L},Theta^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: Theta^right_{m,L}=C^{m+1}xC^{n(L-1)} and Theta^left_{m,L}=C^{m+1}xC^{n(L-1)}, with coordinates (gamma,rho,c) and (delta,sigma,d), respectively

symbols.name: Phi^right_{m,L},Phi^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: axis-conditioned simultaneous binary-form maps from the corresponding Theta spaces to C^{q_L}

symbols.name: C^right_{m,L},C^left_{m,L}
symbols.def: Zariski closures of the images of Phi^right_{m,L} and Phi^left_{m,L} in C^{q_L}

symbols.name: R^right_m(t),R^left_m(t)
symbols.def: fibers of Phi^right_{m,K} and Phi^left_{m,K} over t

symbols.name: J^right_m,J^left_m
symbols.def: Jacobians of Phi^right_{m,K} and Phi^left_{m,K} with respect to their p_K parameter coordinates

symbols.name: Omega^right_m,Omega^left_m
symbols.def: points t having a representation theta in R^right_m(t), respectively R^left_m(t), at which some p_K by p_K minor of J^right_m(theta), respectively J^left_m(theta), is nonzero

symbols.name: F^right_{m,K},F^left_{m,K}
symbols.def: real loading-and-cumulant lists that are realizable by centered non-Gaussian sources with finite K moments in the forward and reverse axis parameterizations

symbols.name: U^right_m,U^left_m
symbols.def: regular opposite-arrow-separation regions in the forward and reverse cumulant-image varieties

symbols.name: V^right_m,V^left_m
symbols.def: nonempty real moment-feasible parameter regions mapped into U^right_m and U^left_m, respectively

symbols.name: A_m,I_m,E_m
symbols.def: A_m is the forward/reverse compatibility system, I_m its elimination ideal in t, and E_m=V(I_m)

symbols.name: S_m
symbols.def: selector based only on nonemptiness of the two fiber correspondences

symbols.name: D(P)
symbols.def: causal arrow between X and Y under P

standard.name: bivariate linear acyclic model with latent confounders

definitions.name: Forward bivariate LvLiNGAM class
definitions.construction: {P in Laws(R^2) : ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:forward-axis-model, and ass:forward-noncollinearity hold for 0<=j<=m+1 and 1<=i<=m}

definitions.name: Reverse bivariate LvLiNGAM class
definitions.construction: {P in Laws(R^2) : ass:independent-sources, ass:finite-cumulants, ass:source-nongaussianity, ass:reverse-axis-model, and ass:reverse-noncollinearity hold for 0<=j<=m+1 and 1<=i<=m}

definitions.name: Stacked joint-cumulant truncation
definitions.construction: T_L(P)=(kappa_{r,a}(P):2<=r<=L,0<=a<=r), where kappa_{r,a}(P)=Cum_P(X repeated r-a times,Y repeated a times) and q_L=L(L+3)/2-2.

definitions.name: Forward simultaneous binary-form map
definitions.construction: [Phi^right_{m,L}(gamma,rho,c)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}c_{jr}(u_{j1})^(r-a)(u_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j) for 1<=j<=m, and u_{m+1}=(0,1).

definitions.name: Reverse simultaneous binary-form map
definitions.construction: [Phi^left_{m,L}(delta,sigma,d)]_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}d_{jr}(v_{j1})^(r-a)(v_{j2})^a for 2<=r<=L and 0<=a<=r, with v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1) for 1<=j<=m, and v_{m+1}=(delta,1).

definitions.name: Axis-conditioned decomposition correspondences
definitions.construction: R^right_m(t)={theta in Theta^right_{m,K}: Phi^right_{m,K}(theta)=t} and R^left_m(t)={eta in Theta^left_{m,K}: Phi^left_{m,K}(eta)=t}.

definitions.name: Jacobian-regular image regions
definitions.construction: J^right_m(theta)=d Phi^right_{m,K}(theta), J^left_m(eta)=d Phi^left_{m,K}(eta); Omega^right_m={t: exists theta in R^right_m(t) with a nonzero p_K by p_K minor of J^right_m(theta)} and Omega^left_m={t: exists eta in R^left_m(t) with a nonzero p_K by p_K minor of J^left_m(eta)}.

definitions.name: Real moment-feasible parameter regions
definitions.construction: F^right_{m,K}={(gamma,rho,c) in R^{m+1} x R^{n(K-1)}: rho_i are pairwise distinct and rho_i!=gamma for 1<=i<=m, and for every 0<=j<=m+1 there exists a centered non-Gaussian real random variable S_j with E|S_j|^K<infty and Cum(S_j repeated r times)=c_{jr} for every 2<=r<=K}; F^left_{m,K}={(delta,sigma,d) in R^{m+1} x R^{n(K-1)}: sigma_i are pairwise distinct and sigma_i!=delta for 1<=i<=m, and for every 0<=j<=m+1 there exists a centered non-Gaussian real random variable S_j with E|S_j|^K<infty and Cum(S_j repeated r times)=d_{jr} for every 2<=r<=K}.

definitions.name: Axis-conditioned Waring-alternative compatibility system
definitions.construction: A_m(t;gamma,rho,c,delta,sigma,d) consists of t_{r,a}=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}c_{jr}(u_{j1})^(r-a)(u_{j2})^a=sum_{j=0}^{m+1}d_{jr}(v_{j1})^(r-a)(v_{j2})^a for every 2<=r<=K and 0<=a<=r, with u_0=(1,gamma), u_j=(1,rho_j), u_{m+1}=(0,1), v_0=(1,0), v_j=(sigma_j,1), and v_{m+1}=(delta,1).

definitions.name: Complex compatibility locus
definitions.construction: I_m=(A_m) intersect C[t_{r,a}:2<=r<=K,0<=a<=r] and E_m=V(I_m) in C^{q_K}; equivalently E_m is the Zariski closure of the t-projection of the complex solution set of A_m.

definitions.name: Separate-fiber direction selector
definitions.construction: S_m(t)=X->Y when R^right_m(t) is nonempty and R^left_m(t) is empty; S_m(t)=Y->X when R^left_m(t) is nonempty and R^right_m(t) is empty; S_m(t) is undefined otherwise.

definitions.name: Worked compatibility systems
definitions.construction: For m=1 set K=4,n=3 in A_m, with j=0,1,2 and r=2,3,4. For m=2 set K=6,n=4 in A_m, with j=0,1,2,3 and r=2,3,4,5,6.

definitions.name: Real exceptional-locus atlas handle
definitions.construction: Use subresultants of the common-direction annihilating polynomial induced by A_m together with real-root and truncated-moment inequalities for (c_{jr},d_{jr}) to stratify E_m(R) and enumerate local real branches of R^right_m and R^left_m.

definitions.name: Real lower-order twin construction handle
definitions.construction: Solve the two axis-conditioned Waring systems through K_-=2m+1 over distinct real loading directions, then realize the resulting finite source-cumulant lists by compactly supported non-Gaussian laws through a truncated-moment-matrix perturbation.

statements.statement: For every m>=1, there are nonempty Zariski-open sets U^right_m subset Omega^right_m minus E_m and U^left_m subset Omega^left_m minus E_m such that, for every t in U^right_m, R^right_m(t) is a singleton modulo permutations of indices 1,...,m and R^left_m(t) is empty, and symmetrically for every t in U^left_m. Moreover, there are nonempty relatively Euclidean-open sets V^right_m subset F^right_{m,K} and V^left_m subset F^left_{m,K} such that Phi^right_{m,K}(V^right_m) is contained in U^right_m and Phi^left_{m,K}(V^left_m) is contained in U^left_m. Thus every real forward-class law whose real loading-and-cumulant parameter list lies in V^right_m satisfies S_m(T_K(P))=D(P)=X->Y, and symmetrically for reverse-class laws. At m=1 this gives fourth-order-truncation orientation on a nonempty real moment-feasible open region.
statements.justification: The result derives complex generic fiber uniqueness and proves that its separation region has a nonvacuous real, moment-feasible population realization.
statements.gap: This is a field-tier strict one-order refinement of ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorems 4.4–4.7: its k=m+2 cumulant matrix reaches order 2m+3, whereas this claim concerns the full order-2 through order-2m+2 truncation. CaiGaoHara2025Direction and CaiHara2026SparsestLvLiNGAM target full-DAG recovery, not this fixed-pair finite-order frontier.
statements.consumer: A lower-order orientation statistic reduces the high-cumulant burden in the pairwise source-finding step of SchkodaRobevaDrton2024.

statements.statement: The complex compatibility locus satisfies E_m=C^right_{m,K} intersect C^left_{m,K} and has codimension m in each complex arrow-image variety. The constructible t-projection of the complex solution set of A_m is Zariski dense in E_m, and each point of that constructible set has both a complex forward and a complex reverse representation. In particular, the m=1 system at K=4 has codimension one and the m=2 system at K=6 has codimension two.
statements.justification: The overlap ideal distinguishes genuine structural non-identification from failure of a particular rank diagnostic.
statements.gap: Neither ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction nor ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 gives an arbitrary-m complex opposite-arrow compatibility ideal or its asserted codimension.
statements.consumer: The equations isolate the locus whose real, moment-feasible part must be resolved before restricted testing is attempted.

statements.statement: For every m>=1, can one construct a Euclidean-open family of real forward laws in the stated class and, for each such law, a real reverse law in the stated class with equal T_{K_-} under the two arrows?
statements.justification: A moment-feasible reverse-twin construction is required before the sufficient order can be called an information-order frontier over real LvLiNGAM laws.
statements.gap: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction provides a sufficient rank criterion but no Euclidean-open real opposite-arrow family through order 2m+1; the prior eid_lnsem_cumulant_id record explains why failure of one witness is not a lower bound.
statements.consumer: A positive resolution would rule out every observable cumulant functional through order 2m+1 for this real model class.

statements.statement: Can one derive a finite semialgebraic stratification of E_m(R) that characterizes exactly which complex compatibility solutions correspond to independent real source laws with finite K moments and gives explicit local descriptions of both real fiber correspondences?
statements.justification: Complex generic separation alone does not reveal the real, moment-feasible geometry needed for a usable orientation diagnostic.
statements.gap: SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 establishes generic rank mechanisms for multivariate discovery, not a real semialgebraic atlas of a bivariate opposite-arrow overlap.
statements.consumer: The atlas provides the population geometry needed to define margin-restricted testing and diagnose proximity to a real reverse-arrow twin.

estimand_functional: D(P)=S_m(T_{2m+2}(P)) whenever exactly one of R^right_m(T_{2m+2}(P)) and R^left_m(T_{2m+2}(P)) is nonempty

sampling_model.design: population identification from observational law P
sampling_model.units: independent draws of (X,Y) when estimation is later added
sampling_model.treatment: none; the target is causal direction
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: not part of this exact-identification core

tldr: This field-tier project sharpens the sufficient information order for bivariate causal orientation in an LvLiNGAM with m non-collinear latent confounders. Chen et al.'s order-(2m+3) rank matrix is sufficient, but it does not exploit the common independent-source directions across all lower cumulant orders. The proposed theorem derives generic complex singleton structural fibers and opposite-arrow exclusion from the full truncation through order 2m+2, and proves that this separation has a nonempty relatively open real, moment-feasible parameter region. It also characterizes the codimension-m complex overlap of the two arrow images. Whether real, moment-feasible reverse twins exist through order 2m+1 remains an explicit construction question. The target is direction, not a causal coefficient.

project_justification.gap: Chen et al. provide a sufficient high-order rank test, not complex generic separation from the complete order-2m+2 truncation with a nonvacuous real-feasible region, a complex opposite-arrow overlap ideal, or a real lower-order reverse-twin construction.
project_justification.niche: The bivariate arbitrary-confounder problem has finite-dimensional simultaneous-cumulant geometry that a single Hankel-style matrix does not encode.
project_justification.fill: The project gives a field-tier one-order refinement of the direct anchor by studying the two axis-conditioned fiber correspondences and their compatibility ideal, while establishing a nonempty real moment-feasible separation region and retaining real lower-order minimality as a constructive question.

related_work: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction is the direct comparator: Theorems 4.4–4.7 orient a pair with a k=m+2 cumulant matrix whose largest entry has order 2m+3; this project is a field-tier refinement that tests whether the full one-order-shorter truncation has generic complex separation and a nonempty real moment-feasible open region. ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 is the one-latent cumulant-asymmetry prototype and informs the m=1 reduction, but is not claimed as a competing arbitrary-m order frontier. CaiHuangChenEtAl2023 uses higher cumulants for recursive recovery under a One-Latent-Component condition rather than fixed-pair order calibration. SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 supplies the closest cumulant-rank technique ancestor, but studies recursive multivariate discovery and compatible effect sets rather than a bivariate opposite-arrow overlap. CaiGaoHara2025Direction permits dependent latent-variable DAGs and seeks full-DAG recovery, so it neither supplies nor subsumes an independent-source bivariate order-(2m+2) frontier. CaiHara2026SparsestLvLiNGAM gives finite-sample recovery of a sparsest full DAG with arbitrary latent confounding, but does not calibrate the least population cumulant truncation for a fixed bivariate arrow. Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability explains why any later finite-sample theory must restrict away from the real feasible part of E_m; it does not determine that locus.
honest_scope: The theorem targets generic population separation for real laws in the displayed open sets and a complex compatibility locus under independent non-Gaussian finite-moment LvLiNGAM sources. It does not establish a real information-order lower bound, uniform finite-sample inference, dependent-latent robustness, or direct-effect-coefficient identification. The real-atlas and lower-order-twin questions retain the moment-feasible geometry required for the first two extensions.

bibliography.citation: Wei Chen, Linjun Peng, Zhiyi Huang, Haoyue Dai, Zhifeng Hao, Ruichu Cai, and Kun Zhang (2025), Identification of Causal Direction under an Arbitrary Number of Latent Confounders, arXiv:2510.22711.
Wei Chen, Zhiyi Huang, Ruichu Cai, Zhifeng Hao, and Kun Zhang (2024), Identification of Causal Structure with Latent Variables Based on Higher Order Cumulants, AAAI 38, 20353-20361.
Ruichu Cai, Zhiyi Huang, Wei Chen, Zhifeng Hao, and Kun Zhang (2023), Causal Discovery with Latent Confounders Based on Higher-Order Cumulants, ICML.
Daniela Schkoda, Elina Robeva, and Mathias Drton (2024), Causal Discovery of Linear Non-Gaussian Causal Models with Unobserved Confounding, arXiv:2408.04907.
Ming Cai, Penggang Gao, and Hisayuki Hara (2025), Causal Discovery for Linear DAGs with Dependent Latent Variables via Higher-order Cumulants, arXiv:2510.14780.
Ming Cai and Hisayuki Hara (2026), Learning Sparsest Linear Causal DAGs with Latent Confounders via Higher-Order Cumulants, arXiv:2607.05984.
Konstantin Genin (2021), Statistical Undecidability in Linear, Non-Gaussian Causal Models in the Presence of Latent Confounders, NeurIPS 34.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Theorems 4.4-4.7 identify direction with a k=m+2 cumulant matrix whose entries reach cumulant order 2m+3.
A higher-cumulant asymmetry criterion provides an early latent-variable causal-direction prototype.
Higher cumulants support recursive causal discovery under a One-Latent-Component condition.
Higher-order cumulant rank matrices support recursive latent-confounded discovery and finite compatible effect sets.
Higher-order cumulants identify full linear DAGs while allowing dependent latent variables.
A finite-sample higher-cumulant method recovers the sparsest linear causal DAG with an arbitrary number of latent confounders.
Unrestricted finite-sample orientation is statistically undecidable under latent confounding.

literature_checklist.author: Chen et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Arbitrary-latent bivariate orientation by an order-(2m+3) cumulant-matrix rank criterion.

literature_checklist.author: Cai et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Recursive higher-cumulant discovery under a One-Latent-Component condition.

literature_checklist.author: Schkoda, Robeva, and Drton
literature_checklist.one_line: Higher-order cumulant rank methods for latent-confounded LiNGAM discovery.

literature_checklist.author: Cai, Gao, and Hara
literature_checklist.one_line: Higher-cumulant full-DAG discovery with dependent latent variables, rather than a bivariate independent-source order frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Cai and Hara
literature_checklist.one_line: Finite-sample sparsest-DAG recovery under arbitrary latent confounding, without a least-order bivariate population characterization.

literature_checklist.author: Genin
literature_checklist.one_line: Latent-confounded LiNGAM orientation lacks unrestricted finite-sample decidability.
